displayTitle: Every Flaw Signs Its Own Name
kicker: PT LEVEL II // INDICATIONS BY MATERIAL FORM

beats.narration: A Level Two reads an indication and asks what process left it. Shape, bleed, and location together name the flaw.

payload.title: EVERY FLAW SIGNS ITS NAME
payload.sub: Shape over time, bleed volume, distribution.

beats.narration: Watch how it forms. A crack draws a line from its first minute; a pore blooms as a dot. A row of pores fakes a linear.
When in doubt, run the bleedback check. Wipe, redevelop, watch it re-form. A crack returns as one line; aligned pores return as separate beads.
And every product form leaves a signature. A forging lap folds metal over without fusing — faint, wavy, oxide-lined at the flash line.

payload.heading: The process writes the flaw
payload.note: LAP · BURST · SEAM — ORIENTATION FOLLOWS THE METAL FLOW

beats.narration: So read the signature, then ask the part. Castings show cold shuts; plate hides laminations until a cut edge. Even glass and plastics take penetrant.

payload.sub: Where the metal flowed tells you which surface to cut and check.
